affected_groups: Currently enrolled pupils and their families
Poorer villages and Roma families, who may be excluded from genuine negotiation input
Parents needing predictability for family and schooling decisions
content.decision_question: Subsidiarity and parental/community agency in tension with predictability, transparency, and equitable access to school continuity information
content.evidence_boundary: Contested and largely unknown: whether multigrade pedagogy systematically affects learning outcomes for disadvantaged pupils is empirically underdetermined in this record, and whether local negotiations in practice include or exclude poorer villages and Roma families (white-flight-style dynamics) is not settled — both are flagged by the raising voice itself as conditions that could reverse their support
content.tension: Keeping retention decisions at the local, negotiated level respects subsidiarity, parental agency, and the school's role as a community institution, protecting enrolled cohorts from disruptive mid-stream change.
content.title: Decision tension A3: S1
value_poles: Subsidiarity and parental/community agency in tension with predictability, transparency, and equitable access to school continuity information
A competing public value requiring human judgment
